dhp290:1: If, by forsaking a limited ease,
dhp290:2: he would see an abundance of ease,
dhp290:3: the enlightened man would forsake the limited ease
dhp290:4: for the sake of the abundant.
dhp291:3: Intertwined in the inter- action of hostility,
dhp291:4: from hostility he’s not set free.
dhp292:1: In those who reject what should,
dhp293:1: But for those who are well-applied, constantly,
dhp293:2: to mindfulness immersed in the body;
dhp293:3: don’t indulge in what shouldn’t be done;
dhp296:4: is constantly immersed in the Buddha.
dhp297:4: is constantly immersed in the Dhamma.
dhp298:4: is constantly immersed in the Sangha.
dhp299:4: is constantly immersed in the body.
dhp300:3: whose hearts delight, both day & night,
dhp301:3: whose hearts delight, both day & night,
dhp302:1: Hard is the life gone forth, hard to delight in.
dhp302:2: Hard is the miserable householder’s life.
dhp302:3: It’s painful to stay with dissonant people,
dhp304:3: The bad don’t appear even when near,
dhp304:4: like arrows shot into the night.
dhp305:4: he’d delight alone– alone in the forest.
